description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

This epic touches Rust command execution, TauRPC generated bindings, Effect service loading, React state coordination, accessibility semantics, and WebDriver documentation. A final quality gate ensures the separately implementable child slices land as one coherent feature and do not leave stale terminology, generated bindings, or tests behind.

This task is intentionally last and should not add major new product behavior. It validates that the feature matches the parent epic's decisions and that the repo is ready for future work without needing to rediscover the original plan.

## What to build

Perform a feature-wide regression pass after the implementation slices are complete. Fix integration fallout, stale generated artifacts, test drift, and documentation mismatches that are too cross-cutting for any single earlier task.

Review the implementation against the parent epic's key goals:

- Beadwork remains the source of truth.
- Ready/Blocked come from Beadwork CLI views.
- Issue List Views are selectable lenses, not combinable filters.
- Search is local, scoped, ID/title/raw-description-only, and non-persistent.
- True-empty, base-empty, and search-empty states are distinct.
- Selection never shows an Issue hidden from the visible list.
- Dependency display remains consistent across views.

## Technical Approach

Run the project's relevant checks and fix failures. At minimum, expect to run the frontend test suite, Rust tests, formatting/linting checks, TauRPC binding generation checks, and the focused WebDriver suite. Use the repo's Ultracite guidance for TypeScript/React formatting and lint cleanup.

Audit docs and names for terminology drift: use Issue, Issue List, Issue Detail, Issue Search, Issue List View, Issue Status, All Issues, Ready, Blocked, and Current Workspace. Avoid stale references to "States" for lifecycle status and stale claims that the Issue Explorer only calls `list_issues`.

## Considerations

- Do not use this task to redesign the feature. If major behavior changes are needed, update the appropriate child issue or create a follow-up.
- Generated TauRPC bindings should be committed if they change.
- Keep backward-compatible commands/docs only when intentional; otherwise remove or mark stale material.
- Ensure no child task's tests are skipped or left as `.only`/`.skip`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Frontend unit/component tests pass.
- [ ] Rust adapter/RPC tests pass.
- [ ] Generated TauRPC bindings are current and committed if changed.
- [ ] Formatting/lint checks pass or all remaining findings are documented with a clear reason.
- [ ] Focused WebDriver issue-list scenario passes locally or any upstream/environmental caveat is documented.
- [ ] Docs and code terminology match the domain glossary and parent epic decisions.
- [ ] No stale test/docs references imply search/filter coverage is still out of scope after this feature lands.

## Test Plan

- Run `pnpm test` or the repo's current frontend test command.
- Run `pnpm dlx ultracite check` and fix or document findings.
- Run `cargo test` in `src-tauri`.
- Run the Issue List WebDriver build/suite commands documented in `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`.
- Inspect `git diff` for generated binding and docs consistency before committing/closing the epic work.
- Run `bw list --parent bsm-dbh --all` and inspect the child issue graph so any follow-up work, deferred scope, or known caveat is recorded before closing the final quality gate.
title: Run feature-wide filtering and search regression quality gate
